title: Dortmanna gibbosa
intro: Dortmanna gibbosa, also known as Marsh St. John's Wort, is a perennial aquatic plant that belongs to the Primulaceae family, typically found in marshy areas and wetlands. The health benefits of this plant include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Dortmanna gibbosa include iridoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and topical creams, which are used to treat various skin conditions and promote wound healing.
taxonomy: Dortmanna gibbosa is a species of flowering plant in the family Lythraceae. It belongs to the genus Dortmanna, which is part of the larger family of loosestrifes. The classification of Dortmanna gibbosa is further broken down into the kingdom Plantae, followed by the division Magnoliophyta, class Magnoliopsida, and order Myrtales. The plant's classification also includes the family Lythraceae, subfamily Lythroideae, and tribe Lythreae. Dortmanna gibbosa is native to the Americas and has a specific habitat and growth pattern.
